primary.usage: App root background. Every screen sits on this. Apple-neutral dark — no warmth, no cold blue. Chosen for professional neutrality.

secondary.usage: Slightly lifted surfaces — modals, drawers, elevated panels. 16-step lift from primary.

tertiary.usage: Highest surface elevation. Input fields, active card states, hover backgrounds.

default.usage: Card and panel background. Sits 7 steps above primary background. Creates gentle depth without harsh contrast.

container.usage: Container elements inside cards — stop list items, passenger rows, metadata sections.

elevated.usage: Modals, dropdowns, tooltips. The highest z-index surfaces.

primary.usage: THE single accent color. Used on: primary CTA button, route line, matched status badge, fare amount, active states. Desaturated warm teal — calm confidence. Contrast ratio ~5.2:1 on #1C1C1E.

dim.usage: Accent color wash for backgrounds — matched state card background tint, hover states on accent elements.

strong.usage: Pressed/active state of accent elements. Darker than primary accent for interaction feedback.

primary.usage: Main body text, headings, critical information. Near-white with slight warmth — easier on eyes than pure #FFFFFF.

secondary.usage: Supporting text — co-rider names, stop addresses, metadata labels. 60% of primary text prominence.

tertiary.usage: Placeholder text, disabled states, timestamps. Lowest hierarchy text — still readable but clearly receded.

on_accent.usage: Text placed ON the accent color background (buttons, badges). Dark enough for contrast — not pure black.

success.usage: TRIP_COMPLETED state, successful actions, positive confirmations. Sage green — calm resolution, not excited green.

success_dim.usage: Success state background wash — Summary Screen ambient tint.

warning.usage: Re-queue notifications, co-rider cancellation banners, expiry warnings. Amber — attention without alarm.

error.usage: Destructive actions only — Cancel Trip button, cascade warning modal. Apple-system red. Used sparingly.

error_dim.usage: Error state backgrounds, cancel modal tint.

info.usage: Intentionally same as accent. Info notifications use accent color — consistent visual language.

default.usage: Default borders on cards, inputs, dividers. Subtle — 1px only.

focus.usage: Keyboard focus ring. Accent color — visible, consistent with interactive identity.

subtle.usage: Between-section dividers inside a card. Barely visible separation.

pending.usage: PENDING status badge color. Grey — waiting, neutral, not alarming.

matched.usage: MATCHED status badge. Accent color — the positive confirmation moment.

active.usage: TRIP_ACTIVE badge. Same as matched — continuity of the positive state.

completed.usage: TRIP_COMPLETED badge. Shifts to sage green — journey done, different from in-progress teal.

cancelled.usage: CANCELLED badge. Tertiary text color — muted, closed, not alarming.

typography._note: Three font families. Each has a job. Never swap them.

display.usage: Screen titles, emotional headline moments, the big matched confirmation. Expressive — has character. Tells the user this was designed with care.

body.usage: All UI text — labels, body copy, form inputs, co-rider names, addresses. Disappears. Readable at 13px.

data.usage: Numbers only — fare amounts, distances, ETA minutes, pendingCycles counter. Monospace makes data feel precise.

display_large.usage: Trip Complete heading, matched confirmation. One per screen maximum.

display_medium.usage: Waiting Screen primary message — 'Finding your ride...'. Sets emotional tone.

display_small.usage: Screen section titles where expressiveness matters.

headline.usage: Card titles, screen headings, important section labels.

title.usage: Sub-section headers, co-rider section label, stop list header.

data_hero.usage: The fare amount ₹47.40, ETA number. The most important number on screen. Large, precise, impossible to miss.

data_large.usage: Secondary data numbers — distance in km, pendingCycles.

body_large.usage: Primary body text — descriptions, form field values, notification messages.

body.usage: Standard body text — co-rider names, stop addresses, helper text.

label.usage: Form labels, metadata keys, status badge text, section labels.

caption.usage: Timestamps, version numbers, legal text. Minimum size — never go smaller.

spacing._note: Base-4 system. Every value is a multiple of 4. Never use arbitrary px values.

1.usage: Micro gaps — icon to label, badge internal padding

2.usage: Tight internal spacing — between two related labels

4.usage: Standard component padding — card internal, input padding

5.usage: Comfortable section spacing within a card

10.usage: Large section gaps — between top content and action area

radius._note: Soft but not bubbly. Material Expressive uses considered rounding — not zero, not circle.

md.usage: Standard cards — the most common radius

lg.usage: Large cards, modal containers, main content panels

xl.usage: Bottom sheet, full-width cards on smaller viewports

elevation._note: Elevation via color tint, NOT box-shadow. Material You principle. Dark surfaces get lighter as they elevate.

motion._philosophy: Motion communicates state change, not decoration. Every animation answers: what just changed and why does it matter?

fast.usage: Hover states, focus rings, badge color changes

standard.usage: Card appearance, status badge transitions, screen fade

expressive.usage: Matched state reveal, trip complete transition — the emotional moments

slow.usage: Loading skeleton shimmer, route animation initialization

standard.usage: Default for most transitions. Material standard easing.

decelerate.usage: Elements entering the screen — slides in and settles.

accelerate.usage: Elements leaving the screen — starts slow, exits fast.

expressive.usage: The matched moment — slight spring overshoot. Subtle celebration.

waiting_animation.description: Two dots — pickup and drop — connected by a dashed line. Dashes animate along the path continuously. Communicates searching without illustrating it.

matched_reveal.description: Trip card scales from 0.96 to 1.0 with opacity 0 to 1. Uses expressive easing for subtle spring feel.

state_transition.description: Screen exits fade down slightly (translateY 8px, opacity 0). New screen fades up (from translateY 8px to 0, opacity 1). Communicates forward progression.

rules: Never animate layout properties (width, height, top, left) — always transform
Only animate transform and opacity for performance
Waiting animation must be pauseable — users on low-power mode should not be punished
No animation longer than 800ms in the main user flow
The matched moment gets the expressive easing — it is the only screen transition that springs

layout._note: Single column for all screens except Trip Screen. Map always right panel on desktop.
